summary: postinstall exfiltrates AWS EC2 IMDS IAM credentials to webhook.site
details: package.json declares a postinstall script that runs at npm install and queries the EC2 instance metadata service (IMDSv2 token at 169.254.169.254, then /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ to retrieve the IAM role's temporary AWS credentials) and POSTs the result to a hardcoded attacker-controlled webhook.site endpoint (path '/?real_aws_keys=1'). The same postinstall unconditionally POSTs status telemetry (no_token/no_role/creds_err/token_err) to that endpoint even when IMDS is unreachable, confirming the destination is attacker infrastructure used for exfiltration and tracking. Package metadata is placeholder-shaped (description 'a normal package', empty author), consistent with a disposable malicious publish. Installing this package on an EC2 host or CI runner with an attached IAM role hands the role's temporary access keys to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
